authBoundaries.name: Operator role semantics
authBoundaries.purpose: Defines the stable operator roles and their purposes that belong to the Product Core.
authBoundaries.expectedInputs: auth provider claims, group memberships, or scopes
authBoundaries.expectedOutputs: mapped GrantLayer operator role and capability assignments
authBoundaries.productionRequirement: roles must be explicitly defined and enforced; no default or demo roles
authBoundaries.notes: Auth provider adapters must map into these stable roles without redefining them.

authBoundaries.name: Capability groups
authBoundaries.purpose: Defines the stable capability boundaries that operator roles may exercise.
authBoundaries.expectedOutputs: allowed or denied capability with audit reference
authBoundaries.productionRequirement: capability checks must be enforced at every protected boundary
authBoundaries.notes: Auth provider adapters must not redefine capability groups; they map provider claims into them.

authBoundaries.name: Auth provider adapter
authBoundaries.purpose: Translates concrete auth provider mechanics into Product Core operator identities and roles.
authBoundaries.expectedInputs: OAuth token, JWT, SSO assertion, mTLS certificate, or API key
authBoundaries.expectedOutputs: validated operator identity with stable role mapping
authBoundaries.productionRequirement: provider must be explicitly configured, validated, and fail-closed if unreachable
authBoundaries.notes: OAuth, JWT, SSO, and mTLS are all valid future adapter targets. Product Core must not depend on one.

authBoundaries.name: Role/capability mapping helper
authBoundaries.purpose: Translates operator roles into allowed and denied capabilities for enforcement.
authBoundaries.expectedInputs: operator role from the auth provider adapter
authBoundaries.expectedOutputs: list of allowed capabilities and denied capabilities
authBoundaries.productionRequirement: mapping must be deterministic, versioned, and auditable
authBoundaries.notes: May be implemented as a configuration-driven lookup table validated on startup.

authBoundaries.name: Operator bootstrap mechanism
authBoundaries.purpose: Creates the first operator/admin in a fresh deployment with secure role assignment.
authBoundaries.expectedInputs: explicit bootstrap configuration with operator identity and role
authBoundaries.expectedOutputs: bootstrapped operator record with audit trail
authBoundaries.productionRequirement: must require explicit configuration; must not use hard-coded or demo defaults
authBoundaries.notes: Bootstrap is a one-time or carefully controlled process. Re-bootstrap should require elevated proof.

authBoundaries.name: Service-to-service identity
authBoundaries.purpose: Provides stable identities for internal and external service workflow execution.
authBoundaries.expectedInputs: service credentials from an explicit service identity provider
authBoundaries.expectedOutputs: attributable service operator record with scoped capabilities
authBoundaries.productionRequirement: must use explicit service identity provider; must not use demo tokens
authBoundaries.notes: external_workflow_agent and service_operator roles depend on this boundary.

authBoundaries.name: Auth audit event integration
authBoundaries.purpose: Feeds authentication and authorization events into the Product Core audit stream.
authBoundaries.expectedInputs: auth success, auth failure, access denied, role assignment change
authBoundaries.productionRequirement: all operator-sensitive actions must produce auditable records
authBoundaries.notes: Audit records must never contain plaintext tokens, JWTs, or provider-specific secrets.

authBoundaries.name: Production fail-closed enforcement
authBoundaries.purpose: Ensures production mode refuses to start if required auth configuration is missing or invalid.
authBoundaries.expectedInputs: runtime mode, auth provider configuration, operator bootstrap state, role mapping state
authBoundaries.expectedOutputs: startup allowed or explicit structured error with no secret leakage
authBoundaries.productionRequirement: must refuse startup if any required auth config is missing, invalid, or uses demo defaults
authBoundaries.notes: Fail-closed behavior must be validated by integration tests before any production claim.

authBoundaries.name: Local/test/demo auth shortcuts
authBoundaries.purpose: Provides non-production authentication convenience for developers, testers, and demonstrators.
authBoundaries.expectedInputs: local-dev, test, or demo runtime mode flag
authBoundaries.expectedOutputs: simplified auth behavior with explicit non-production labeling
authBoundaries.productionRequirement: must be explicitly disabled in production; must fail closed if enabled
authBoundaries.notes: Shortcuts are acceptable only when runtime mode is not production and is explicitly declared.

authBoundaries.name: OpenAPI security contract
authBoundaries.purpose: Documents auth requirements in the OpenAPI contract for integrator and client clarity.
authBoundaries.expectedOutputs: OpenAPI security schemes, scopes, and protected path annotations
authBoundaries.productionRequirement: contract must reflect actual enforcement; must not document unimplemented protections
authBoundaries.notes: Security contract updates must be versioned and reviewed like any other contract change.

providerAdapterExpectations: Product Core should not depend on one specific auth provider.
OAuth, JWT, SSO, and mTLS can all be implemented as future provider adapters.
Local and demo tokens are non-production shortcuts only.
Production must use explicit provider configuration validated on startup.
Provider claims must map into stable GrantLayer roles and capabilities.
Provider-specific fields must not leak into Product Core records, audit logs, or evidence bundles.
failClosedRules: Production mode must refuse to start if auth provider is not explicitly configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if operator bootstrap configuration is missing.
Production mode must refuse to start if role/capability mapping is not configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if demo or default admin tokens are present.
Production mode must refuse to start if local/test/demo auth shortcuts are enabled.
Production mode must refuse unauthenticated access to any protected endpoint.
Denied access must return a structured error without leaking configuration details or secrets.
Missing auth configuration must produce a clear, auditable error record.

localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: Synthetic operator IDs used in automated tests for deterministic behavior.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: Test identities provide no real authentication and must not be present in production.

localDemoTestShortcuts.shortcut: Local admin token for developer convenience
localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: A simple shared token for local-dev environments to reduce setup friction.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: Shared tokens provide no identity verification, role separation, or auditability.

localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: A preconfigured operator for walkthroughs and stakeholder demonstrations.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: Preconfigured demo identities bypass real authentication and authorization checks.

localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: Synthetic service accounts loaded from test fixtures for integration tests.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: Fixture identities are synthetic and must not be deployed to production environments.

localDemoTestShortcuts.purpose: A preconfigured readonly account for demo integration queries.
localDemoTestShortcuts.productionRisk: Demo readonly accounts lack real authentication and must not access production data.

productionRequiredConfiguration: Auth provider endpoint and validation rules
Operator bootstrap configuration with at least one valid admin
Service identity provider configuration for workflow agents
OpenAPI security contract alignment with enforcement
auditExpectations: Grant approval actions must produce auditable records with actor, timestamp, and policy reference.
Evidence mutation actions must produce auditable records with hash references and actor identity.
Policy changes must produce auditable records with before/after summaries and actor identity.
Permission and role assignment changes must produce auditable records with actor and justification.
Auditor export access must produce auditable records with scope, timestamp, and actor identity.
Audit records must never contain plaintext tokens, JWTs, passwords, or provider-specific secrets.
External workflow agent actions must be attributable to a stable operator or service identity.
